title: QA validation: coverage, container DB, Chromium E2E, Stitch §4.2 fidelity
description: qa-agent runs the full quality bar for todo-mvp after the data-plane iterate slice: PostgreSQL via Docker Compose + Prisma as the default persistence path; HTTP JSON APIs for todos and activity feed; idempotent seed and test DATABASE_URL for integration/E2E; frontend routes consume APIs per tasks 21–24. Enforces 100% line coverage on declared unit and integration scopes (integration jobs container-only with test-scoped DATABASE_URL); automated-manual Chromium E2E over every MVP-visible behavior; mandatory Stitch §4.2 fidelity with diff_ratio 0 pass rule; deterministic seed and/or STITCH_BASELINE_UI documented where needed so API-backed data does not destabilize raster baselines.
acceptance: Unit coverage is 100% line for the full declared unit scope; CI configuration matches this requirement and is verified on the integration branch or release candidate.

Integration coverage is 100% line for the full declared integration scope with tests executed only inside isolated containers; scope includes Prisma repository adapters and HTTP Route Handlers from tasks 19-backend-prisma-repositories-idempotent-seed and 20-backend-http-json-api-todos-activity-feed (and related infrastructure modules per architecture-brief coverage_scope_exclusions).
E2E and integration jobs use a test-scoped DATABASE_URL pointing at the Postgres service in the compose stack for that job—never a developer workstation default or shared prod-like instance.
Quickstart path is verified: compose Postgres up, prisma migrate (or documented equivalent), idempotent seed applied, app serves HTTP API and UI reads todos/feed via API.

Chromium E2E suite passes and covers all MVP-visible behaviors: todo create, list, toggle, edit, delete with confirmation, empty state, error/retry UX for failed load/save, four-route navigation, Dashboard todos from API, Activity Feed from persisted API (not default fixture file path), Task Creation (Dark) persisting via API, Social Login stub behavior unchanged in intent.
E2E run uses the containerized stack documented in infra tasks; no sampling of MVP behaviors unless discovery explicitly narrows scope.

Stitch fidelity §4.2 (mandatory; design_package.json stitch.source_of_truth and requires_stitch_fidelity_qa): for every shipped screen in design_package.json stitch.screens[] (ids 11611814ea7a4a2881d76c0c95fd3066, ed27f21d8f24440a940099c842b535bf, eeb35234b6764d199f5085e69e9cc0fc, 0caac050ee504d6ebca41d202bbd1ca7), qa-agent captures the running app in Chromium at the viewport width × height documented in projects/todo-reference/tasks/todo-mvp/07-ui-generator-stitch-four-screens/artifacts/ui_spec.json (or architecture-approved stitch_fidelity_report.json viewports[]).

For each compared screen, run an automated raster/pixel diff against projects/todo-reference/design/stitch/screens/<screen_id>.png; store both the app capture and the Stitch reference under projects/todo-reference/tasks/todo-mvp/quality-gate/artifacts/visual/ (e.g. app-<screen_id>.png and stitch-ref-<screen_id>.png) for audit.

Emit projects/todo-reference/tasks/todo-mvp/quality-gate/artifacts/stitch_fidelity_report.json including at least: status; compared_baseline (path to projects/todo-reference/design/stitch/meta.json or equivalent); summary viewports[]; per_screen[] entries each with id, stitch_png_path, app_screenshot_path, viewport, comparison_tool, diff_ratio, pass, mismatches[].

Pass rule: overall status pass only if every per_screen[].pass is true with diff_ratio 0 (no differing pixels / tool-equivalent zero) per architecture-brief (no stitch_qa_pixel_diff waiver); any non-zero diff or missing per-screen row ⇒ fail.

Even when diff_ratio is 0, list ui_spec section-order / primary-affordance violations in per_screen[].mismatches[] by comparing to projects/todo-reference/tasks/todo-mvp/07-ui-generator-stitch-four-screens/artifacts/ui_spec.json.
When design_package.json sets acceptance.requires_design_visual_acceptance true: QA produces qa_visual_evidence.json and companion captures under artifacts/visual/ per testing skill §4.1.
QA report includes attributable browser console issues and relevant failed network requests for the app under test.
Structured QA output provides validation_status pass|fail and issues list suitable for engineering-manager handoff.
Declared coverage scope exclusions (if any) stay aligned with Vitest include globs and are recorded under coverage_scope_exclusions in tasks/todo-mvp/architecture-brief.json.
implementation_notes: QA retry 2026-04-19: re-run full gate after infra E2E image, frontend Stitch/a11y, stitch-fidelity-report ui_spec mismatches.
